todos.task: v2 architecture: rewrite as a proper multi-file trip-agnostic web app after CAN26 trip
todos.ref: Current app is ~5200 lines single file, hardcoded 2026/Oct dates, global state, inline event handlers, regex-parsed compound strings. Good data model and timezone logic — these survive into v2. v2 scope: split HTML/CSS/JS, trip-agnostic data model, structured fields everywhere (closes TODO #92), proper event delegation, no global state mutations. Decision: build v2 after Oct 2026 trip using real usage as a guide.

todos.task: V2 architecture spec: split single-file app into engine + data + devlog
todos.ref:
Design decision captured post-v0.9.86. Current single-file approach (5,700+ lines, 330KB) is at the upper comfortable bound. Any new feature touching schema or DEFAULT_DATA should be treated as a trigger to begin the split. 

V2 target structure: 
(1) app.html — markup + app shell + UI logic only.

(2) styles.css — all styling.

(3) data.[tripname].json — all trip-specific data: itinerary, flights, hotels, restaurants, packing, user todos, settings. Engine loads this on first run, normalizes, persists to localStorage. Subsequent loads use localStorage as authoritative — no historic migrations on every load.

4) devlog.json or external Markdown — developer todos and changelog, decoupled from runtime. Key design principles: schemaVersion in metadata drives discrete vN→vN+1 migration registry (not a monolith). 

DEFAULT_DATA becomes a trip template, not a seed database embedded in code. Reuse model: duplicate data.[tripname].json for each new trip — engine is untouched. 

Trip loader concept: switch datasets from same engine. Entanglement risk: syncDefaultTodos, migrateData, renderAll, buildCalendarEvents all have implicit knowledge of data shape — defining a clean engine/data interface is the core design challenge for v2, not just moving files. 

Dev log todos currently serve as changelog and session memory between AI sessions — moving them out requires replacing that function deliberately (candidate: external CHANGELOG.md + session brief). 

Timeline: do not attempt before trip. Natural start point is post-CAN26 when picking up for next trip.

todos.task: Save PDF backup of each 3×5 reference card
todos.ref: Use the '⬇ Save PDF' button in the 3×5 Cards dialog. Select a card, click Save PDF — app generates a 2-page PDF (front + back) and downloads it automatically as CAN26_Card_[Name].pdf. Store in iCloud Drive and Google Drive (drive.google.com/drive/folders/1adhCX8PRqY5cnE1Tpap-q0uD0HifulHt). Do this for all 5 cards once final content is verified.

packingNotes.text: What is plan for Non-US destinations?
Need a more structured backpack than the Eddie Bauer.
Uniqlo sweater worked out very well. In general, the merino wool shirts worked well.
Didn't use any of the travel hangars.
The alpaca and Darn Tough socks were too thick for the Skechers. Good thing I bought the thinner merino wool socks.
Plastic belts worked ok for the most part. However, at the Pentagon they made us take them off and it was rather clumsy. The black plastic buckle belt is too hard to resize.
The wall outlet multiplug is really useful. It might be better to find one with a flatter profile.
Need unlimited data plan on vacations.
Patagonia pants may be too lightweight for colder climates. Tech pants work well.
Things I missed on a previous trip: Toothbrush, Microfiber cloth for glasses, Apple Pencil sleeve, Casual clothes for hotel room (T-shirt, sweat pants).

archivedTodos.task: HST label missing on HNL-origin flights — toHomeTzLabel returned null when fromTzid === homeTz
archivedTodos.ref: Fixed v0.9.43 — removed early null return. All timed events now show 🏠 HST label regardless of whether local timezone is already HST. Consistent across flights, hotels, trains, custom events.

archivedTodos.task: tzFromCityStr false-matched 'HST' in 'PDT (HST+3)' offset notation, returning Pacific/Honolulu for Vancouver hotels
archivedTodos.ref: Fixed v0.9.44 — now only matches HST as primary tz token (when PDT/PST absent). Root cause: Sheraton/Kamloops checkin field says 'Time zone: PDT (HST+3)' meaning local is PDT. Old code saw 'HST' and returned Honolulu tzid, causing wallTimeToUTC to convert 4PM as if it were HST, producing wrong UTC and wrong Google Calendar display time (4PM HST instead of 1PM HST).

archivedTodos.task: ICS export used UTC-Z timestamps — Google Calendar displayed local city time as if it were HST
archivedTodos.ref: Fixed v0.9.44 — switched all timed events (flights, hotels, custom) to DTSTART;TZID=[city_tz]:local_time format. Google Calendar now has the city timezone, converts correctly to viewer timezone (HST), and can display both local and HST times. e.g. Sheraton check-in: DTSTART;TZID=America/Vancouver:20261004T160000 → Google shows 1:00 PM HST.

archivedTodos.task: ICS SUMMARY: append local time + tz abbreviation to event title
archivedTodos.ref: Implemented v0.9.45 — e.g. '[CAN26] 🏨 Check-in: Sheraton Vancouver Wall Centre (4:00 PM PDT)'. Extracted from DTSTART;TZID= field so it always matches the actual stored time. Only added when event has a TZID timestamp (timed events); all-day events unchanged.

archivedTodos.task: Restaurants: upgraded to first-class confirmed reservation entity
archivedTodos.ref: v0.9.46 — added confirmed (bool), reservationTime (HH:MM), confirmationNum, partySize fields. Confirmed dinners emit timed TZID-based calendar events with HST label, matching hotel/flight behavior. Unconfirmed remain all-day events. migrateData backfills new fields. restaurantForm shows toggled confirmed section. renderRestaurants shows ✅ CONFIRMED badge with time/conf#/party.

archivedTodos.task: Confirmed dinner exported as all-day in ICS — reservation time input was plain text, regex silently failed
archivedTodos.ref: Fixed v0.9.47 — switched input to type=time (browser enforces HH:MM, regex always matches). Added warning toast in ICS fallback path for confirmed dinners so future silent failures surface. Root cause: silent all-day fallback with no feedback is a recurring fragility pattern across all timed event types.

archivedTodos.task: Time inputs: enforce type=time everywhere, remove dead fallbacks, add required validation
archivedTodos.ref: v0.9.48 — custom event and restaurant time inputs all use type=time (browser enforces HH:MM). Removed dead 'else { displayTime = e.time }' fallback in buildCalendarEvents. Simplified custom event save validation from regex to empty check. Added required-time guard in restaurant save when confirmed=true.

archivedTodos.task: Fix: mobile sidebar overlay persists after resizing window past 900px breakpoint

Fixed in v0.9.52 by adding a window resize listener that calls closeSidebar() whenever innerWidth > 899.

archivedTodos.task: Fix: migrateData changes not written back to file handle on load
archivedTodos.ref: applyLoadedData() called saveToLocalStorage() only — not saveState(). So migrated defaults (new todos, backfilled fields) were in memory and localStorage but never written to the linked .json file. Next session the file loaded again without them. Fixed v0.9.53: applyLoadedData now calls saveState() so file handle gets updated immediately after migration.

archivedTodos.task: Fix: new default todos not appearing after upgrade — migrateData timing unreliable
archivedTodos.ref: migrateData ran correctly but multiple load paths (file handle, localStorage, btnNew) meant migrated state wasn't always persisted. Root fix in v0.9.54: syncDefaultTodos() runs inside renderAll() on every startup — safe, idempotent, catches all load paths. Supersedes the applyLoadedData saveState() fix from v0.9.53.

archivedTodos.task: Fix: syncDefaultTodos id-only matching caused false collision with user-created todos
archivedTodos.ref: User todos assigned by nextId() incremented into the same id range as new default developer todos (96–100). syncDefaultTodos saw those ids as already present and skipped injection. Fixed v0.9.55: match on id+group composite key ('id:group') — a user todo with a colliding numeric id is not a match unless the group also matches.

archivedTodos.task: Todo id namespace: developer and user todos share the same integer id space — collisions visible in UI
archivedTodos.ref: Won't fix / by design. syncDefaultTodos() ensures all developer todos (up to #154) are always present in state.todos before nextId() runs on any new user todo, so user todos always land at #155+. The two populations are naturally separated. Composite-id scheme deferred indefinitely — v2 would start from a clean architecture anyway (see #134).

archivedTodos.task: 3×5 cards: popup window causes duplex print failure on Epson ET-8500; CSS background silhouette not printed
archivedTodos.ref: v0.9.91 — (1) Replaced window.open/document.write with Blob URL download: generates CAN26_Cards.html, triggers download, toast says open in Safari or Preview. Eliminates browser popup rendering race that caused incomplete duplex jobs. (2) Moved mountain silhouette from CSS ::after pseudo-element (suppressed by print engines) to inline SVG via cardMountain() helper injected into all 10 card functions. (3) Updated in-file instructions to: open in Safari/Preview, set paper to 3×5in, enable duplex.

archivedTodos.task: 3×5 cards: output PDF not HTML; duplex still only printing back page
archivedTodos.ref: v0.9.92 — Replaced intermediate HTML approach with html2canvas + jsPDF pipeline. printCards() downloads CAN26_CardGen.html which: (1) loads html2canvas 1.4.1 + jsPDF 2.5.1 from cdnjs CDN, (2) waits for document.fonts.ready, (3) renders each .card-page div to canvas at scale:3 (~288dpi), (4) assembles into a multi-page PDF with 5x3in landscape pages, (5) saves CAN26_Cards.pdf. Progress bar shown during generation. PDF opened in Preview/Safari handles duplex correctly since it is a proper multi-page document.

archivedTodos.task: 3×5 cards: generate PDF directly on button click; fix mountain silhouette missing from PDF
archivedTodos.ref: v0.9.93 — (1) Eliminated intermediate HTML file entirely. printCards() is now async, loads html2canvas + jsPDF via loadScript() into main page (once, cached), creates fixed-pixel hidden container in main DOM (480x288px = 5x3in @96dpi), renders each card one at a time, assembles jsPDF and calls pdf.save('CAN26_Cards.pdf') directly. Button label updates to 'Rendering N/total…' during generation. (2) Mountain silhouette replaced from SVG (which html2canvas renders inconsistently) to [canvas class='card-mountain-canvas'> drawn by drawMountainCanvas(). Called explicitly on each card before html2canvas capture. Canvas drawing is always captured natively.

archivedTodos.task: 3×5 cards: mountain silhouette flooding card text — barely visible
archivedTodos.ref: v0.9.94 — Root cause: drawMountainCanvas path arrays ended with 'W,12,W,H' (pixel values) but were being divided by 360 and 28, producing x=640 and y=617 on a 480×36 canvas. Path closure from those out-of-bounds coords created a massive dark fill region covering the whole card. Fix: rewrote both ridge paths entirely in coordinate space (0–360 x, 0–28 y) with no pixel variable mixing. Used x()/y() helper functions. Front ridge peaks also lowered (y values 14–26 not 12–24) to keep silhouette subtle at card bottom only.

archivedTodos.task: 3×5 cards: headers/footer clear but body text barely visible and too large
archivedTodos.ref: v0.9.95 — Root cause: card CSS (.card-page, .card-body, .card-row, etc.) was only defined inside buildCardWindow HTML string, which is no longer used now that rendering happens in the main DOM. Cards rendered with browser default styles — font too large, colors wrong. Fix: added getCardCSS() returning pixel-unit card styles (px not pt/in to avoid dpi ambiguity) injected as [style tag] into container before each card. Updated html2canvas target from container.firstElementChild to container.querySelector('.card-page') to skip injected style tag. Also added 'Confirmation:' prefix to QWRVFP and DIR1117775 card-ref labels.

archivedTodos.task: 3×5 cards: card size less than full 3x5 in PDF; Preview duplex only prints one page; AS 2355/56 confusing; footer barely visible
archivedTodos.ref: v0.9.96 — (1) Render container moved from position:fixed;left:-9999px (causes layout issues in some engines) to position:fixed;top:0;left:0;opacity:0;z-index:-1 — guarantees proper layout computation. (2) Footer/side-label color darkened from #C8C0B4 to #6B6058. (3) AS 2355/56 split into two rows: AS 2355 SEA→YVR (outbound) and AS 2356 YVR→SEA (return), both noting Horizon Air. (4) PDF page order changed from interleaved front/back to all-fronts-first then all-backs — print fronts, flip stack, print backs. No duplex setting needed, avoids Preview duplex confusion entirely.

archivedTodos.task: 3×5 cards: text not filling card size in PDF; black border visible; revert to interleaved duplex
archivedTodos.ref: v0.9.97 — (1) Border removed from .card-page in getCardCSS() — added in v0.9.90 as design idea, not wanted. (2) jsPDF orientation:'landscape' removed — was fighting format:[5,3] and could swap dimensions; now using format:[5,3] directly with no orientation. pdf.addPage() likewise uses [5,3] without orientation string. (3) Removed explicit width/height override from html2canvas options — let it use element's actual rendered dimensions. Settle timeout increased from 60 to 120ms. (4) Reverted page order to interleaved front/back; duplex to be revisited separately.

archivedTodos.task: 3×5 cards: PDF generating as portrait 3x5 instead of landscape 5x3 — content fills only top portion, scale 78%
archivedTodos.ref: v0.9.98 — Root cause: removing orientation:'landscape' in v0.9.97 caused jsPDF to default to portrait and silently swap [5,3] to [3,5]. Screenshot showed Portrait selected, Scale to Fit at 78%, content in top 60% of page. Fix: restored orientation:'landscape' to both jsPDF constructor and addPage() call. With landscape, jsPDF treats [5,3] as 5-wide x 3-tall correctly. Preview Auto Rotate should now align the page properly.

archivedTodos.task: 3×5 cards: revert to popup window approach; PDF approach never matched original edge-to-edge sizing
archivedTodos.ref: v0.9.99 — Photo comparison showed original popup window cards filled 3x5 edge-to-edge at native print resolution; PDF approach consistently showed white margins and scaling issues. Reverted to window.open + document.write + @page{size:5in 3in landscape;margin:0}. Dropped all html2canvas/jsPDF infrastructure. SVG silhouette restored (works in popup at print resolution). Duplex solved with on-screen instructions: Odd pages only (fronts) → flip stack → Even pages only (backs). getCardCSS/loadScript/drawMountainCanvas/async printCards all removed.

archivedTodos.task: 3×5 cards: odd/even page selection in Preview dialog unreliable with Epson rear tray; replace with two separate print jobs
archivedTodos.ref: v1.0.00 — Replaced single 'Open Print Window' button with two buttons: 'Print Fronts' (btn-secondary, navy/gold) and 'Print Backs' (btn-primary). Each opens its own popup with only those pages — fronts-only window or backs-only window. printCards(side) accepts 'fronts' or 'backs'. buildCardWindow(pages, totalCards, label) shows context-appropriate instruction: fronts window says flip stack and print backs; backs window confirms fronts should already be done. Each is a clean single-sided print job — no duplex setting needed at all.

archivedTodos.task: Adopt 3-level versioning scheme: M.mm.nnnn
archivedTodos.ref: v0.10.100 — Scheme: MM=major (manual), mm=minor (manual), nnnn=build (incremented each rev). No leading zeros. Display: '0.10.100'. File naming: CAN26_Planner_vM_mm_nnnn.html. Starting at 0.10.100 — the 100 acknowledges prior work. v1.0.0 is reserved for first production release (trip data locked, cards verified, no open P1 bugs).

archivedTodos.task: 3×5 cards: prints edge-to-edge with no margins; landscape resets in system dialog
archivedTodos.ref: v0.10.101 — (1) Added margin:0.08in to @page rule — small clearance so content doesn't bleed to paper edge. (2) Landscape override by macOS system dialog is a known browser limitation — @page size is respected by browser print dialog but macOS resets orientation when escalating to system dialog. Workaround: set landscape once in system dialog and save to '3x5 BW Rear Tray' preset.

archivedTodos.task: 3×5 cards: @page margin:0.08in causes card to overflow printable area — only gray line printed
archivedTodos.ref: v0.10.102 — Root cause: .card-page is width:5in height:3in but @page margin:0.08in shrinks printable area to 4.84×2.84in — card overflows and browser renders almost nothing. Fix: reverted @page to margin:0, instead increased card internal padding from 0.17/0.22/0.14/0.22in to 0.22/0.28/0.18/0.28in. Full 5×3 page used, content pushed away from edges via padding.

archivedTodos.task: Import JSON dialog — button label mismatch: showed 'Delete' instead of 'Import' or 'Archive'

archivedTodos.task: Hotels: sort by checkinDate at render time instead of relying on array order
archivedTodos.ref: Hotels added via UI are always appended to end of array, breaking chronological display order. Fix: sort hotels by checkinDate in renderHotels() before building HTML. One-line sort — no schema change required. Workaround until fixed: export JSON, reorder manually, reimport. First surfaced when Hotel #7 (Kamloops return Oct 11–12) was added after Hotel #6 (Vancouver Oct 12–13).

archivedTodos.task: Itinerary: city field missing from all days in JSON export/import cycle
archivedTodos.ref: DEFAULT_DATA itinerary days have a city field but migrateData() only backfills top-level keys — it never iterates per-day fields. Result: city was absent from all itinerary days in state and JSON exports. Fixed v0.11.0: migrateData() now matches each saved itinerary day to its DEFAULT_DATA counterpart by day field and backfills city (and dayMeals) if undefined.

archivedTodos.task: Itinerary: dayMeals missing from all days in JSON export/import cycle
archivedTodos.ref: Same root cause as #208 — migrateData() never backfilled per-day fields on itinerary records. dayMeals (breakfast/lunch/dinner with desc and provided fields) was present in DEFAULT_DATA but absent from saved state and JSON exports for all days. Fixed v0.11.0 alongside #208: single per-day backfill loop in migrateData() restores both city and dayMeals from DEFAULT_DATA on first load after upgrade.

archivedTodos.task: Itinerary: add UI to edit row detail text (time, activity, detail, meals fields per row)
archivedTodos.ref: Itinerary row content originated from early Apple Notes imports and could not be edited in the app. Fixed v0.11.2: added ✏️ button to each itinerary row. editItineraryRow(di,ri) opens a modal with fields for time, hst, activity, detail (textarea), and meals. Saves to state.itinerary[di].rows[ri] and re-opens the day panel after save. Dirty-form guard and dirty-dot indicator work automatically via existing openModal() infrastructure.

archivedTodos.task: Itinerary: day numbers too faint — rgba(0,0,0,0.07) nearly invisible
archivedTodos.ref: Fixed v0.11.4 — .day-num color bumped from rgba(0,0,0,0.07) to rgba(0,0,0,0.20). Visible as a watermark number without competing with the day label text.

archivedTodos.task: Itinerary: departure day (Oct 3) label 'day: —' renamed to 'Pre-Trip'
archivedTodos.ref: Fixed v0.11.4 — DEFAULT_DATA departure day changed from day:'—' to day:'Pre-Trip'. renderItinerary now detects non-numeric day values and renders them at 13px bold instead of 32px to fit the header space cleanly.

archivedTodos.task: Itinerary rows: replace freeform Local Time text field with structured date/time/tz picker
archivedTodos.ref: Fixed v0.11.4 — added rowDate (YYYY-MM-DD), rowTime (HH:MM 24h), rowTz (IANA tzid) fields to each row. Edit and Add Row modals now show a structured time fieldset (date+time+tz pickers in a 3-column grid) alongside a freeform Display Label override. migrateData backfills rowDate/rowTime/rowTz as '' on all existing rows. rowLocalTime() computes the time-column display from structured fields; falls back to row.time if not set.

archivedTodos.task: Itinerary rows: HST field should be computed from structured time + home TZ setting, not manually entered
archivedTodos.ref: Fixed v0.11.4 — rowHstLabel() computes home-TZ label from rowDate+rowTime+rowTz via toHomeTzLabel() when structured fields are set. Falls back to legacy row.hst string for rows without structured time. HST field removed from edit modal — no longer manually entered. Consistent with how flights and calendar events compute home-TZ labels.

archivedTodos.task: Packing list sata model considerations for next app rev
archivedTodos.ref: Data model note: Categories and sub-categories only exist as properties on items — there's no separate category list. So "add category" just stores a named category on a __placeholder__ item (invisible), or more cleanly, we store a state.packing[person].categories array. The cleanest approach without touching the data model is to derive categories from items — an "empty" category simply isn't possible without a placeholder, so Add Category will create the category name in the dropdown but not persist it until an item is added to it. I'll handle this by adding a state.packing[person].cats and state.packing[person].subs arrays to store user-defined category/sub names.

archivedTodos.task: GitHub Error
archivedTodos.ref:
[03/23/2026 5:06:16 AM] pushToGitHub
iPhone: GitHub push failed 403: Unable to validate Restricts updates to workflow files: Rule was unable to be completed in 10 seconds

Diagnosis (Mar 23): GitHub repo has a branch ruleset "Restrict updates to workflow files" enabled. When this rule is active, GitHub requires the PAT to have the `workflow` scope even when not touching workflow files — without it the rule evaluation fails with 403. Fix: GitHub → Settings → Developer settings → Personal Access Tokens → find CAN26 token → edit → add `workflow` scope.
